name: Cañón del Río Lobos, Senda de las Gullurías y Ermita de San Bartolomé
name_original: Cañón del Río Lobos, Senda de las Gullurías y Ermita de San Bartolomé
region_intro: Hiking around Ucero offers diverse landscapes within the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park. The region features an impressive canyon with geological formations, rolling hills, and dense forests. Winding rivers, including the Ucero and Lobos, provide picturesque riverside walks. The terrain supports varied flora and fauna, making it suitable for exploration across different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Ucero The most popular hiking route is Cañón del Río Lobos, Senda de las Gullurías y Ermita de San Bartolomé, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours to complete. This moderate route explores the canyon and leads to the historic Ermita de San Bartolomé. Another top favourite among local hikers is Source of the Ucero River – Valdecea Pond and Cliffs loop from Ucero, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.5 km) path. This trail follows the Ucero River to Valdecea Pond and its surrounding cliffs. Local hikers also love the Valdecea Pond and Cliffs – Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew loop from Ucero, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through natural ponds and historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours. Hiking around Ucero is defined by deep canyons, winding rivers, and dense forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging routes through varied terrain. The routes in Ucero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Ucero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ucero?
A: There are nearly 20 hiking routes around Ucero, offering diverse experiences within the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging excursions through varied terrain.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for hiking trails in Ucero?
A: You'll find a good mix of difficulty levels. The area offers 7 easy routes, 9 moderate routes, and 3 more difficult trails, ensuring there's something suitable for every hiker, from beginners to experienced adventurers.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes in the Ucero area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Ucero are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17543337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ucero River – Valdecea Pond and Cliffs loop from Ucero&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38273122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecea Pond and Cliffs – Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew loop from Ucero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Ucero?
A: Hiking in Ucero means exploring the stunning Cañón del Río Lobos, known for its impressive geological formations carved by the Río Lobos. You'll also encounter rolling hills, dense forests, and winding rivers like the Ucero and Lobos. Keep an eye out for diverse native flora and fauna along the trails.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes incorporate historical sites. You can visit the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucero Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is freely open to visitors and integrated into routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40384209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Lobos – Ucero Castle loop from Ucero&lt;/a&gt;. The Roman Tunnel, part of an ancient water delivery system, is another intriguing feature on some paths, though an alternative route is available for those who prefer to avoid it.

Q: Where can I find the best viewpoints in the Ucero area?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views of the canyon, forests, and surrounding mountains, head to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3634900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. This viewpoint is accessible via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056697384/canon-del-rio-lobos-senda-de-las-gullurias-y-ermita-de-san-bartolome" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón del Río Lobos, Senda de las Gullurías y Ermita de San Bartolomé&lt;/a&gt; trail, which is a popular moderate route.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Ucero?
A: Yes, Ucero offers several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40384209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Lobos – Ucero Castle loop from Ucero&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.7 km trail that takes you past historical sites and along the river, making it a great option for a family outing.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Ucero?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 180 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty of the Cañón del Río Lobos, the diverse landscapes, and the well-marked trails that cater to all abilities.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Ucero?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not listed, Ucero is a popular destination within the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park, and it's generally well-equipped for visitors. You can typically find parking near the visitor center or main access points to the park's trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ucero?
A: The diverse landscapes of Ucero and the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer is popular for longer days. Winter hiking is also possible, though conditions may vary.

Q: Are there any trails that follow the river?
A: Yes, the region is characterized by winding rivers. The main walking route in the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park is the River Trail, which follows the course of the River Lobos for 25 km. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17543337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ucero River – Valdecea Pond and Cliffs loop from Ucero&lt;/a&gt; follows the Ucero River, offering picturesque riverside walks.
tips: Going up to the Gullurías viewpoint will allow us to have a different and higher perspective of the natural park.

Cueva Grande is a natural cave located in the Cañón del Río Lobos natural park, specifically in the area of the Templar hermitage of San Bartolomé. It has numerous legends and has also been used to film numerous reports and films. It is an idyllic place full of peace.

Located in a unique setting, isolated at the mouth of the Lobos River Canyon, this beautiful construction was part of a Templar monastery, San Juan de Otero, of which only the chapel remains. The architectural symbols inscribed on its walls have been the source of numerous debates, attributing some to them of esoteric origin.
